title: Close out epic e7c: run epic retro and write the roadmap completion report
description: Meta-work tick (orchestrator-owned). e7c is the FINAL epic in the soft-ordering roadmap (4bt -> o16 -> e7c), so this close-out is retro-only — there is no next epic to flesh out. Run the epic-close retro per references/claude-runner.md, promote/record learnings in .tick/learnings.md, then write the roadmap completion report and yield to the human (two-tier stopping rule: the roadmap end is the hard stop). Include in the report: whether the e7c->o16 edge should retroactively have been an after edge, as a real-world datapoint on the preference-vs-dependency distinction this roadmap shipped.
acceptance_criteria: retro run and learnings recorded per claude-runner.md; roadmap completion report written; e7c closed
closed_reason:
Epic e7c retro and close-out. All 5 implementation ticks closed; final review passed (2 blockers found and fixed before close).

WHAT SHIPPED:
- iga: tk graph now accounts for cross-epic open blockers; missing blockers treated as closed (consistent with ready.go); external open blocker → not agent_ready, wave ≥2.
- zlc: TUI roadmap-native selection — j/k/up/down navigate epics in roadmap mode; Enter jumps to the epic in tree mode, expanding collapsed ancestors and relaxing filters; selection survives reload.
- 6ii: Web board editing for after edges — tick-create-dialog and tick-detail-drawer gain "After (soft order)" inputs; cloud and local transports handle create/update; full server round-trip.
- h3l: .tick/config.md added — PR+CI gate policy + build rules for implementers.
- Hook: .claude/settings.json PreToolUse guard blocks writes to installed skill copies (.claude/skills/).
- Model-tier rule: claude-runner.md now makes model= explicitly required on every Agent call.

REVIEW FINDINGS (both fixed before close):
- B1: Hook read file_path from wrong JSON level (top-level vs tool_input); block messages went to stdout not stderr. Fixed: correct nesting + stderr redirect. Lesson promoted to claude-runner.md "Hook input shape" note.
- B2: CloudCommsClient.updateTick built a partial replacement tick, clobbering parent/type/notes/created_at on the cloud path. Fixed: merge updates into tickCache base before sending. Pre-existing vy0 transport debt now mitigated at the call site.

e7c→o16 PREFERENCE-VS-DEPENDENCY DATAPOINT (per yj6 spec):
The e7c epic was originally hard-blocked on o16. After 4bt shipped the after field, we dogfooded it by converting the edge to after at the 4bt close-out — e7c appeared with status active (not queued) on the roadmap, exactly as the feature intends. Retroactively the e7c→o16 coupling was pure preference ("deliver surfaces before follow-ups"), not a technical dependency. The only real technical coupling was at the task level: zlc was blocked_by o16 because both modified internal/tui/roadmap.go (same-file sequencing rule). That fine-grained distinction — epic preference edge vs. task-level technical edge — is the canonical live example of what the after field is for.

ROADMAP STATUS: 4bt → o16 → e7c — all three epics closed. Roadmap complete.
Branch claude/multi-epic-roadmap-ticks-8eqpiv pushed; PR + CI gate required before merging to default branch per .tick/config.md.
